hadiths.text_en: Actions are judged by intentions; every person gets what they intended.
The best of you are those who learn the Quran and teach it.
Purity is half of faith.
None of you truly believes until he loves for his brother what he loves for himself.
The strong man is not the one who wrestles others down; he is the one who controls himself when angry.
Make things easy and do not make them difficult.
The best of deeds is the prayer performed on time.
Speak good or remain silent.
Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day should honor his guest.
The merciful are shown mercy by the Most Merciful. Be merciful to those on earth.
Smiling at your brother is charity.
The world is a prison for the believer and a paradise for the disbeliever.
Do not waste water, even if you are at a flowing river.
A good word is charity.
The most beloved deeds to Allah are those done consistently, even if they are few.
The best of people is the one who is most beneficial to others.
Paradise lies at the feet of mothers.
Do not be angry, and Paradise is yours.
Whoever shows mercy will be shown mercy by the Most Merciful.
Whoever does not show mercy will not be shown mercy.
Spread the greeting of peace; it spreads love among you.
The best of you is the one who is best to his family.
Give charity without delay, for it stands in the way of calamity.
Help your brother whether he is an oppressor or oppressed.
Whoever travels a path seeking knowledge, Allah will ease his path to Paradise.
The upper hand is better than the lower hand; give before you are in need.
Visit the sick, feed the hungry, and free the captive.
The most beloved prayer to Allah after the obligatory is the night prayer.
Every act of kindness is charity.
Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day, let him say good or remain silent.
This world is cursed and what is in it is cursed, except the remembrance of Allah.
Lower your gaze and guard your private parts.
Whoever removes a worldly hardship from a believer, Allah will remove from him a hardship on the Day of Resurrection.
Spread peace among yourselves.
Envy is permitted only for two: one given wealth who spends it rightly, and one given wisdom who acts by it.
The greatest sins are associating partners with Allah, disobeying parents, murder, and false testimony.
Whoever is not merciful to our young or respectful to our elders is not one of us.
The Prophet never criticized food; if he liked it he ate it, and if he disliked it he left it.
The first thing a person will be questioned about on the Day of Resurrection is prayer.
Cleanliness is half of faith; praise of Allah fills the scale.
Fast and you will be healthy.
Islam is built on five pillars: testimony of faith, prayer, zakat, fasting, and pilgrimage.
Whoever guides others to good will have a reward like those who do it.
The best charity is what is given when you are healthy and hopeful.
Kindness is never found in something but it adorns it.
Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day should not harm his neighbor.
The sign of a hypocrite has three traits: when he speaks he lies, when he promises he breaks it, when trusted he betrays.
Fear Allah wherever you are, follow a bad deed with a good deed to wipe it out.
A Muslim is the one from whose tongue and hand other Muslims are safe.
Do not envy one another, do not hate one another, and be brothers.
The remembrance of Allah is the best of deeds.
The one who recites the Quran proficiently will be with the noble, obedient angels.
The most complete of the believers in faith are those with the best character.
Be in this world as though you were a stranger or a wayfarer.
Allah did not send down any disease but He also sent down the cure.
There is a cure in three things: a drink of honey, cupping, and cauterization; but I forbid my nation from cauterization.
Two words are light on the tongue, heavy on the scale, beloved to the Most Merciful: Glory be to Allah the Almighty.
Whoever builds a mosque for the sake of Allah, Allah will build for him a house in Paradise.
Take advantage of five before five: your youth before your old age, health before sickness, wealth before poverty, free time before busyness, and life before death.
Do not speak ill of the dead, for they have reached what they sent forth.
Allah does not look at your bodies and appearances, but He looks at your hearts and deeds.
Maintain ties of kinship, even if others cut them.
Remember death often, for it destroys pleasures.
The best of your days is Friday.
Avoid that which makes you doubt and stick to that which does not make you doubt.
Allah loves that when one of you does a deed, he does it with excellence.
Allah the Almighty said: I am as My servant thinks of Me.
Fulfilling a promise is a sign of faith.
The most burdensome prayers for the hypocrites are the Isha and Fajr prayers.
The most beloved of people to Allah are those who are most beneficial to people.
Do not conceal the faults of a Muslim brother; Allah will conceal your faults on the Day of Judgment.
Paradise is surrounded by hardships and Hell is surrounded by desires.
Allah records good and bad deeds; whoever intended to do good and did it gets ten rewards.
Subhanallah fills the scales.
Whoever recites Ayat al-Kursi after every prayer will enter Paradise.
The best remembrance is La ilaha illallah, and the best supplication is Alhamdulillah.
Hold fast to the Quran and Sunnah and you will never go astray.
Do not harm and do not be harmed.
The greatest trial is that of the prophets, then the righteous, then those who follow them.
The believer is a mirror to his fellow believer.
Whoever dies knowing that there is no god but Allah will enter Paradise.
Allah does not take away knowledge by removing it from the hearts of people, but takes it away by the death of scholars.
The best of speech is the Book of Allah, and the best of guidance is the guidance of Muhammad.
Attend the Friday prayer, for it is a command of Allah.
If you have surplus food, share it with those who have none.
Verily, deeds are judged by their endings.
Whoever abandons the world for the sake of Allah, Allah will compensate him with better.
Showing mercy to animals is also a form of charity.
Allah is gentle and loves gentleness in all things.
Verily, with hardship comes ease.
Whoever calls to guidance will have a reward equal to those who follow him.
The best of you are those who are best in repaying debts.
Do not cut off relations, do not turn away from one another, do not hate one another, and be servants of Allah as brothers.
He who believes in Allah and the Last Day should honor his guest.
The Children of Israel were led by prophets; whenever a prophet died, another prophet succeeded him.
The strong believer is better and more beloved to Allah than the weak believer.
The Prophet was not indecent or obscene; he used to say that the best among you are those who are best to their wives.
Treat women well, for they were created from a rib.
Every child is born in a state of fitrah (natural disposition).
The deceased is punished in his grave for the wailing of his family over him.
Whoever relieves a believer of a worldly grief, Allah will relieve him of a grief on the Day of Resurrection.
Seek knowledge even if it is in China.
When a man dies, his deeds come to an end except for three: a continuous charity, knowledge that is beneficial, or a righteous child who prays for him.
Avoid the seven destructive sins.
The Muslim has five duties toward his fellow Muslim: return his salams, visit him when he is sick, follow his funeral, accept his invitation, and say yarhamukallah when he sneezes.
Guard your duty to Allah and He will guard you.
The best prayer after the obligatory ones is the night prayer.
Do not say bad things about time, for Allah is time.
Make things easy for people, not difficult, and give glad tidings, do not make them run away.
Whoever sees an evil must change it with his hand; if he cannot, then with his tongue; if he cannot, then with his heart.
Beware of suspicion, for it is the most lying form of speech.
The believer does not get stung from the same hole twice.
Verily, truthfulness leads to righteousness, and righteousness leads to Paradise.
Backbiting is to mention about your brother what he would dislike.
None of you has faith until I am more beloved to him than his children, his father, and all people.
I have been commanded to fight people until they say La ilaha illallah.
Whoever conceals a Muslim's fault, Allah will conceal his fault in this world and the hereafter.
Between a man and shirk and kufr is the abandonment of prayer.
The best jihad is a word of justice before a tyrannical ruler.
Do not belittle any good deed, even meeting your brother with a cheerful face.
The best of Islam is feeding others and greeting those you know and those you don't.
Whoever does not give thanks to people does not give thanks to Allah.
Make things easy; do not make things difficult. Give glad tidings and do not make people run away.
Prayer is a pillar of religion.
Remember Allah standing, sitting, and lying on your side.
Whoever loves to meet Allah, Allah loves to meet him.
Allah accepts the repentance of a servant as long as the death rattle has not yet begun.
The best among you in the pre-Islamic period of ignorance are the best in Islam, if they understand Islamic teachings.
Whoever loves for Allah and hates for Allah has perfected his faith.
Do not sever family ties, do not harbor hatred, and do not spy on one another.
The love of this world is the root of all evil.
Whoever is killed defending his property is a martyr.
Richness is not having many possessions but richness is being content with oneself.
Whoever has three daughters and is patient with them will enter Paradise.
When a person dies, the angels ask: What has he sent forth? And the people say: What has he left behind?
Whoever fasts Ramadan with faith and expecting reward will have all his past sins forgiven.
Whoever stands in prayer during Laylat al-Qadr with faith and expecting reward will have all his past sins forgiven.
The supplication of a fasting person is not rejected.
Allah laughs at two men: one who kills the other, then both of them enter Paradise.
He is not a believer who eats his fill when his neighbor is hungry.
The pen has been lifted from three: the sleeper until he wakes, the child until he matures, and the insane until he recovers.
Give charity, for it will be a shield for you from the Fire.
Supplicate to Allah while being certain of being answered.
Verily Allah does not look to your appearances or your wealth but to your hearts and your actions.
The greatest sin is to associate partners with Allah, then to kill a soul, then to commit fornication.
There is a tree in Paradise called Tuba; a rider can travel under its shade for a hundred years without crossing it.
Allah is pleased with three things for you: that you worship Him alone, that you hold fast to His rope all together, and that you give sincere advice to whoever Allah puts in charge of your affairs.
Whoever recites Surah al-Kahf on Friday will have a light between him and the next Friday.
Observe prayer, give zakat, and obey your rulers, you will enter your Lord's Paradise.
The person who is constantly asked by others for advice has a good character.
Allah created mercy in one hundred parts; He sent down one part to earth and kept ninety-nine.
Whoever does not thank the small blessings will not be thankful for the big ones.
The upper hand is better than the lower hand. The upper hand is the one that gives, and the lower hand is the one that takes.
Patience at the time of a calamity is a virtue.
Whoever covers a Muslim's fault, Allah will cover his fault in this world and the next.
Seven people will be shaded by Allah on the Day when there is no shade except His shade.
Allah says: O son of Adam, so long as you call upon Me and ask of Me, I shall forgive you for what you have done.
Whoever recites the last two verses of Surah al-Baqarah at night, they will suffice him.
The most perfect believers in terms of faith are those with the best character and who are most kind to their wives.
There is no disease that Allah has created, except that He also has created its treatment.
Help your brother when he is an oppressor by stopping him from oppression.
Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day should not harm his neighbor; treat women kindly.
Zakat is obligatory on every Muslim who possesses the nisab for one year.
Pray the five daily prayers, fast Ramadan, give zakat, and perform Hajj.
The one who repents from sin is like one who has not sinned.
The best of charity is when you are healthy and hopeful of a long life.
A man follows the religion of his friend; so each one should consider whom he makes his friend.
Verily, your deeds are presented before Allah twice a week: on Monday and Thursday.
Seeking knowledge is an obligation upon every Muslim.
Do not envy each other, do not artificially inflate prices, do not hate each other.
He who has no mercy will not be shown mercy.
When Allah loves a person He calls Gabriel and tells him that He loves that person.
Whoever prays the dawn prayer in congregation, then sits remembering Allah until sunrise, then prays two rak'ahs, it will be equal to a complete Hajj and Umrah.
Hajj is Arafah.
An accepted Hajj has no reward except Paradise.
Whoever performs Hajj and does not commit obscenity or wrongdoing will return as the day his mother bore him.
The best among you is the one who does not harm others with his tongue and his hands.
The deen is easy; no one makes the deen too hard except that it will overcome him.
Whoever fears the night journey, he reaches his destination at dawn.
The dua of a Muslim for his absent brother is answered.
Supplication is the brain of worship.
There are two blessings that many people are deceived about: health and free time.
Whoever wears silk in this life will not wear it in the Hereafter.
The best charity is that given when you are least wealthy.
The scholars are the heirs of the prophets.
The most virtuous jihad is to speak a word of truth before a tyrannical ruler.
The ink of the scholar is more sacred than the blood of the martyr.
Whoever Allah wants good for, He gives him understanding in the religion.
Verily, Allah is beautiful and He loves beauty.
